mechanism: githubVariables.json defines org, repo, and per-environment variables. A workflow propagates them to GitHub via API. Bicep parameter files consume them at deploy time via readEnvironmentVariable().
why: Without a single committed source, configuration drifts between environments and onboarding a new capability requires tracing values through the GitHub UI.

related.note: Capabilities read shared config without depending on each other's outputs.
New repos receive a subset of platform variables at creation.
Per-environment client IDs sourced from this file.

violations: Bicep parameter with hardcoded tenant ID or location instead of readEnvironmentVariable().
GitHub variable created in UI without matching entry in githubVariables.json.
